what_it_does: Renders the Map / Brief Extraction workspace: a React component that visualizes auto-extracted design maps as SVG overlays on an approved mockup and lists structured outputs such as scene brief, style map, layer map, semantic map, motion map, QA rules, and asset manifest. It supports an embedded mode so another workspace can reuse the same body without its header and trailing note.
observations: Uses window globals (window.MapsWorkspace, window.MapsOverlays, window.MS_DATA, window.ViContext) instead of ES modules.
Provides an embedded prop that suppresses the header and ViNote for reuse by the Build workspace.
Four SVG overlay components (Layer, Semantic, Motion, Interaction) use hardcoded percentage or absolute coordinates.
A tab bar switches which overlay is rendered on top of the mockup image.
Structured map data is displayed in Panel components with edit/add action buttons.
Header actions include Re-extract, Export maps, and Build package navigation.
React hooks are aliased as useStateM and useEffectM.
standouts: Overlay components are exposed as window.MapsOverlays for external reuse.
Embedded mode enables workspace composition without duplicating layout.
SVG overlays are hardcoded rather than generated from map data.
Integrates with window.ViContext to set assistant-aware workspace state.
conventions: Global window namespace for shared symbols.
CSS utility classes and CSS variables alongside inline styles.
Array mapping with .map() for tabs, lists, and overlays.
Conditional rendering based on the embedded prop.
concerns: SVG overlay coordinates are hardcoded and may not match arbitrary mockups.
No PropTypes, TypeScript, or runtime validation for the maps data shape.
Heavy reliance on window globals complicates testing and bundling.
Navigation handlers reference 'package' and 'asset' workspaces without local definition.
Tab buttons lack aria-pressed or other accessibility state indicators.
notes: This is the Maps workspace UI: it overlays hardcoded SVG annotations on an approved mockup and surfaces structured extraction outputs, with an embedded mode for reuse by Build.
questions: How are overlay coordinates derived from the mockup data?
What do the overlay numeric labels and semantic labels correspond to in maps.layer_map and maps.semantic_map?
Is window.ViContext guaranteed to exist whenever embedded is false?
Are maps.motion_map entries plain strings or structured objects?
What do the Re-extract and Export maps buttons actually trigger?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to show extracted layers and briefs on top of an approved mockup; the file does that, adds structured output panels, and supports embedded reuse, so it matches and extends the stated purpose.
intention_signals: The file tries to turn an approved mockup into an editable, inspectable set of design maps that both humans and an AI assistant can reason about, while letting other workspaces embed the same visualization to avoid duplication.
novelty: The combination of tabbed SVG overlay modes for different map types and an embedded mode that exports both the workspace body and its overlays as reusable window globals.
contribution: A concrete UI surface for reviewing and editing auto-extracted design maps, plus reusable overlay components and an embeddable body that downstream workspaces can incorporate without rebuilding the layout.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that renders auto-extracted design maps as SVG overlays on an approved mockup and displays structured brief, style, layer, semantic, motion, interaction, QA, and asset data.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an interactive, assistant-aware review surface for extracted design maps and make its visualization reusable inside other workspaces.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG overlay that outlines numbered rectangular layer regions on the mockup.
symbols.description: Draws dashed gold rectangles at fixed positions over the mockup image and labels each with a numbered circle, visualizing the layer map extraction.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG overlay labeling semantic roles at fixed points on the mockup.
symbols.description: Places gold dots and dark tooltip-style labels such as active, source, evidence, ghost action, and Vi note on the mockup to indicate the semantic meaning of regions.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG overlay showing implied motion paths on the mockup.
symbols.description: Draws dashed curved arrows with arrowhead markers between fixed coordinates to suggest movement or transition flows across the scene.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG overlay highlighting interactive hotspots with pulsing rings.
symbols.description: Displays gold pulsing circles at fixed interaction points, using SVG animate elements to indicate clickable or tappable areas.
